description: The Spanish Cobras is a primarily Latino street gang founded in the 1970s in Chicago, Illinois, by former members of the Maniac Latin Disciples. It operates in multiple Midwestern states and has a strong presence on Chicago's north and west sides. The gang is part of the Folk Nation alliance and is known for its rivalry with People Nation gangs, including the Latin Kings.

country_links.evidence_quote: present in multiple states throughout the Midwestern United States ... They are known to operate in parts of Illinois, as well as in Milwaukee, Racine and Kenosha in Wisconsin, and in Detroit. Law enforcement has also reported Spanish Cobras in Ohio, Connecticut, Orlando, Ft Myers and South Florida.
country_links.context: The Spanish Cobras operate in multiple states throughout the Midwestern United States, including Illinois, Wisconsin, Michigan, Ohio, Connecticut, and Florida.
